objections.claim: The READ-ONLY oracle admits direct write-capability. `openSync` is allowlisted, but the described check validates only imported names and does not constrain call flags; `openSync(path, "w")` can create or truncate a file while satisfying the oracle. Additionally, scanning only `.mjs` permits an imported local `.js` module to use write APIs outside the scan. Therefore an implementation can mutate the repository and still pass `test-readonly.mjs`.

The verification fixtures do not discriminate the specified multi-hop supersession behavior. The mechanism permits `v11 -> v13 -> v15`, but the listed positive golden covers only direct-to-active chains. An incorrect implementation that requires every `superseded_by` to equal `active_plan.version` would pass the golden and every listed negative fixture while rejecting a valid chain.

The node-backed classification is not implementable deterministically because the approach never defines which exact field of a `SUPERSEDED` record is the retirement's “referenced identity.” Such a record can contain multiple 64-hex identifiers (`id`, `superseded_by`, authority or evidence references). Membership testing without a closed extraction rule can classify the same input differently and does not satisfy the machine-readable exact-data requirement.
